text.title: Grafana Provisioning Rules
text.summary: Rules for configuration-as-code provisioning of datasources, dashboards, alerting, and secrets handling in Grafana.

rule.statement: Provision all datasources via YAML files in `/etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources/`; never create them manually in the UI in production.
rule.rationale: UI-created datasources are not version-controlled and are lost on container restart unless the database is backed up. YAML provisioning is idempotent and GitOps-compatible.

rule.statement: Mark all provisioned datasources as `editable: false` in production to prevent UI drift.
rule.rationale: If a user modifies a provisioned datasource in the UI, the change is lost on the next restart when Grafana re-applies the provisioning files.

rule.statement: Inject all credentials (database passwords, API keys) via environment variables referenced in YAML as `${VAR_NAME}`, never hardcoded.
rule.rationale: YAML provisioning files are committed to version control. Hardcoded credentials in Git are a security breach regardless of repository visibility.

rule.statement: Use `foldersFromFilesStructure: true` in the dashboard provider to derive Grafana folder names from the filesystem directory structure.
rule.rationale: Manually specifying folder names for hundreds of dashboards creates maintenance overhead. Filesystem-derived folders are automatic and consistent.

rule.statement: Set `disableDeletion: true` for production dashboard providers and `allowUiUpdates: false` to prevent UI edits from diverging from Git.
rule.rationale: Allows developers to view and explore dashboards without accidentally overwriting the provisioned version. Changes must go through Git to take effect.

rule.statement: Provision alert rules, contact points, notification policies, and mute timings all via YAML — unified alerting must be enabled (`[unified_alerting] enabled = true`).
rule.rationale: Legacy alerting (`[alerting] enabled = true`) is deprecated. Unified Alerting is the only path for provisioned alert management and HA-compatible alert deduplication.

antipattern.description: Mixing manual UI configuration with provisioning files in the same Grafana instance.
antipattern.reason: Provisioning on restart overwrites UI changes silently. Teams discover their manual changes are gone after the next deployment, causing repeated work and confusion.

text.reference: Provisioning YAML for Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, PostgreSQL
Dashboard provider config with folder organization
Alert rules, contact points, policies, mute timings
